title: Project Metadata, Assets, PyInstaller 6.21, Platforms, and Clean Launch · Runtime Lens: Resource Paths
summary: The game depends on the current directory, omits fonts and sound, builds one executable on Linux and calls it cross-platform, lacks licenses, and is never installed fresh. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the resource paths increment in portable inspected game release candidate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Resolve packaged resources through supported APIs instead of current working directory or mutable source paths.
Declare Python and dependency bounds, entry point, package data, license, authorship, version, and reproducible environment metadata.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-resource-paths — Resolve packaged resources through supported APIs instead of current working directory or mutable source paths.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-packaging-assets-platforms-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-packaging-assets-platforms-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_packaging_assets_platforms_2(target_platforms: int = 3, clean_installs: int = 3, declared_assets: int = 12, packaged_assets: int = 12, licenses_recorded: bool = True, offline_launch: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if target_platforms < 1 or clean_installs < 0:
        return False, target_platforms, "package-domain"
    if clean_installs != target_platforms:
        return False, clean_installs, "platform-gap"
    if packaged_assets != declared_assets:
        return False, packaged_assets, "asset-gap"
    if not licenses_recorded or not offline_launch:
        return False, packaged_assets, "release-input"
    assert packaged_assets == declared_assets
    return True, target_platforms, "portable-build"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-resource-paths-h63d0k
# Competency: asteroids-resource-paths.
# Case 5821: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely.
def evidence_asteroids_resource_paths_h63d0k(target_platforms: int = 3, clean_installs: int = 3, declared_assets: int = 12, packaged_assets: int = 12, licenses_recorded: bool = True, offline_launch: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_h63d0k = "h63d0k-k0d36h-5821"
    assert evidence_variant_h63d0k
    evidence_axes_h63d0k = (
        "playerh63d0k",
        "seed5821",
        "inputk0d36h",
        "frameh63d0k5821",
        "state5821h63d0k",
        "playtestk0d36h5821",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_h63d0k)) == 6
    if target_platforms < 1 or clean_installs < 0:
        return False, target_platforms, "package-domain"
    if clean_installs != target_platforms:
        return False, clean_installs, "platform-gap"
    if packaged_assets != declared_assets:
        return False, packaged_assets, "asset-gap"
    if not licenses_recorded or not offline_launch:
        return False, packaged_assets, "release-input"
    assert packaged_assets == declared_assets
    return True, target_platforms, "portable-build"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Resource Paths model · Theory Resource Paths
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Resource Paths: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 6766 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to package code and licensed assets from an identified revision and prove clean offline launch on supported platforms; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Resolve packaged resources through supported APIs instead of current working directory or mutable source paths.
